marketFocus: Trump: 'Iran is negotiating and wants a deal.' Israel: ceasefire possible by Saturday. Iran FM: 'no talks; exchange of messages via mediators is not negotiation.' Iran outlined 5 conditions. Situation fluid.
UK Core CPI 3.2% y/y (beat vs 3.1% exp), Services 4.3% (beat). BOE Greene: 'more worried about inflation risks than demand slowdown.' GBP Bullish — stagflation confirms hawkish BOE stance.
Oil easing on ceasefire hopes: WTI $86.46–$89.57/bbl, Brent $93.45–$97.00/bbl. Barclays month-end: strong USD buying vs most majors. USD Unemployment Claims at 13:30 FFT.

USD.tone: Month-End USD Buying vs Stagflation Drag — DXY Rangebound
USD.summary: USD is Neutral today. DXY traded 99.07–99.56 with Barclays flagging strong month-end USD buying against most majors. However, the stagflation signal from Flash PMIs (Q1 GDP 1.3%, CPI ~4%) and the Navarro/Powell political drama prevent a clean directional bias. Unemployment Claims at 13:30 FFT is the key event. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: DXY rangebound 99.07–99.56; Barclays month-end: strong USD buying vs most majors
Navarro: 'Powell staying at Fed would be absolutely toxic' — political uncertainty
Flash PMIs: Q1 GDP 1.3%, CPI ~4% — stagflation signal caps clean bullish case
USD Unemployment Claims at 13:30 FFT — key remaining catalyst today
Besant reportedly exploring ways to recast Fed-Treasury ties in BOE's image (FT)

EUR.tone: German IFO Beat Fails to Lift EUR — Energy Importer Drag Persists
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today. EUR/USD traded 1.1566–1.1630 despite a German IFO beat (Business Climate 88.6 vs 85.5 exp). IFO Expectations declined sharply (86.0 vs 90.5 prior) — 'economy is feeling the impact of the Middle East situation.' ECB Lagarde: 'as expected deviations from inflation target grow larger and more persistent, the case for action becomes stronger.' Energy importer drag intact. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: EUR/USD 1.1566–1.1630; German IFO beat (88.6 vs 85.5) but Expectations fell to 86.0 vs 90.5 prior
IFO: 'economy is feeling the impact of the Middle East situation'
ECB Lagarde: 'case for action becomes stronger as deviations grow more persistent'
ECB Rehn: 'keeping a cool head; must respond to overall macro environment, not oil alone'
Energy importer: Brent $93–$97/bbl still acts as direct tax on Eurozone growth

GBP.tone: Core CPI Beat + BOE Hawkish Tone — GBP Outperforming
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. UK Core CPI came in at 3.2% y/y (beat vs 3.1% exp) and Services at 4.3% (beat vs 4.2% exp). BOE Greene: 'more worried about risks from higher inflation than slower demand from this shock.' Gilts opened higher by ~35 ticks. GBP/USD traded 1.3358–1.3436, hovering around key DMAs. The stagflation diagnosis now firmly supports the BOE hawkish pivot. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: UK Core CPI 3.2% y/y (beat vs 3.1% exp); Services 4.3% (beat vs 4.2% exp)
BOE Greene: 'more worried about inflation risks than demand slowdown from this shock'
GBP/USD 1.3358–1.3436; Gilts opened higher by ~35 ticks on CPI beat
BOE Greene: 'not tempted to vote for a hike at March meeting' — but tone hawkish
UK Retail Sales tomorrow (07:00 FFT) — next key catalyst for GBP

JPY.tone: Ceasefire Hopes Reduce Haven Demand — USD/JPY Firmer
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY traded 158.56–159.20 as ceasefire hopes reduced haven demand and the dollar rebounded. BOJ Core CPI (06:00 FFT) came in soft, reinforcing the view that the BOJ tightening path is gradual. Yen outperformed higher-beta peers (AUD, NZD) but underperformed vs GBP and CAD. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: USD/JPY 158.56–159.20; ceasefire hopes reduce haven demand for JPY
BOJ Core CPI (06:00 FFT) soft — gradual BOJ tightening path intact
JPY outperformed higher-beta peers (AUD, NZD) but lagged GBP and CAD
Risk-on narrative from ceasefire talks caps haven bid
No major JPY events remaining today — trades on geopolitical sentiment

AUD.tone: Antipodeans Underperforming — CPI Miss Overhang Persists
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. Antipodeans remained softer with AUD underperforming after yesterday's CPI miss (3.7% y/y vs 3.8% exp). Copper firmed slightly on ceasefire hopes ($12,192–$12,348/t) but the FX impact of the CPI miss and reduced May RBA hike odds continues to dominate. ASX 200 closed slightly lower with miners and materials leading declines. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: AUD underperforming after yesterday's CPI miss (3.7% y/y vs 3.8% exp)
May RBA hike odds reduced — Trimmed Mean 0.2% MoM (miss vs 0.3% exp)
Copper firmed slightly ($12,192–$12,348/t) but insufficient to lift AUD
ASX 200 slightly lower; miners and materials front-running declines
No major AUD events today — trades on risk sentiment and metals

CAD.tone: Oil Floor Holding — Iran's 5 Conditions Keep Energy Risk Premium
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. Oil is easing on ceasefire hopes but the floor is holding: WTI $86.46–$89.57/bbl, Brent $93.45–$97.00/bbl. Iran's 5 conditions for ending the war (including no future strikes, compensation, formal Hormuz control) keep the energy risk premium intact. Macquarie forecasts Brent floor at $85–$90/bbl even if tensions ease. CAD remains a pure petro-proxy. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Oil floor holding: WTI $86.46–$89.57/bbl, Brent $93.45–$97.00/bbl
Iran's 5 conditions for ceasefire keep energy risk premium intact
Macquarie: Brent floor $85–$90/bbl even if tensions ease; $150/bbl if Hormuz stays shut
Iran IRGC 'toll booth' regime in Hormuz — supply disruption risk premium intact
No major CAD events today — pure petro-proxy

CHF.tone: SNB Intervention Resistance + Ceasefire Hopes Cap Haven Bid
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. SNB Chairman Schlegel confirmed elevated intervention readiness yesterday. Ceasefire hopes further reduce haven demand for CHF. EUR/CHF stabilizing after the SNB signal. The SNB Quarterly Bulletin today is the key event — any additional intervention language would reinforce the bearish case. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: SNB Schlegel: 'readiness to intervene is elevated; FX interventions more suitable in some situations'
Ceasefire hopes reduce haven demand, reinforcing bearish CHF case
EUR/CHF stabilizing after SNB intervention signal caps appreciation
SNB Quarterly Bulletin today — key event for CHF direction
CHF appreciated 2.5% trade-weighted since December — SNB sees this as excessive

NZD.tone: Underperforming with AUD — No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. NZD underperformed alongside AUD in overnight trade. Westpac flagged Q2 GDP contraction risk due to rising fuel prices. RBNZ Chief Economist Conway: 'monetary policy can't solve the cost-of-living crisis alone.' No domestic hawkish catalyst. High-beta risk proxy with no support. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: NZD underperformed alongside AUD in overnight trade
Westpac: 'NZD GDP set to contract in Q2 due to rising fuel prices'
RBNZ Conway: 'monetary policy can't solve cost-of-living crisis alone'
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
High-beta risk proxy with no domestic hawkish catalyst
